food_name: Fish Cake Sandwich (Bánh Mì Chả Cá)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Vietnamese fish cake sandwich (bánh mì chả cá). Mapped to the broader 'sandwich (food product)' class as the closest defensible FoodOn anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Sandwiches) used as the closest defensible category. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for Vietnamese-style fish cake sandwich.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Vietnamese sandwich preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one whole sandwich weighing approximately 350 g, comprising a full baguette filled with fish cake, pickled vegetables, fresh herbs, and condiments.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — composite estimation from sandwich components: French baguette (FDC ID 172688), fish cake / surimi-style fish products (FDC ID 175168), pickled vegetables, and fresh herbs
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Vietnamese fish cake sandwich (bánh mì chả cá). Nutrient values are estimated by cross-referencing the input record values (ai_generated, confidence 0.7) against USDA FoodData Central component entries for French baguette (FDC ID 172688), fish cake and surimi-style fish products (FDC ID 175168), pickled vegetables, and fresh herbs. The original input data source (GrabFood menu listing) is a forbidden primary source under Project Veritas v2 source policy and is not cited as evidence. USDA FoodData Central is the approved reference anchor for this composite estimate. Values are treated as medium-confidence estimates pending a direct approved-source match.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on white baguette / French bread GI
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vietnamese fish cake sandwich (bánh mì chả cá). GI estimated at 70 based on the dominant carbohydrate component being white French baguette, for which the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008, Diabetes Care) report GI values of 68–75 (mean ~70). Fish cake, pickled vegetables, and herbs contribute negligible carbohydrate and do not materially alter the overall GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 65.1) / 100 ≈ 45.6, rounded to 45.7. Serving carbohydrate: 18.6 g/100g × 350 g = 65.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium from fish sauce, pickled vegetables, and fish cake; estimated 1,200 mg sodium per full sandwich serving
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this sandwich to half a serving or fewer, request fish sauce and pickled vegetables on the side or in reduced quantities, and monitor total daily sodium intake carefully given that a full 350 g serving provides approximately 1,200 mg sodium — more than half the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI refined white baguette dominates carbohydrate load; 65 g carbohydrate per full serving with low fibre (3.9 g) produces rapid postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to half a sandwich, choose a wholemeal or multigrain baguette alternative where available, and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables or protein to blunt the glycemic response from the high-GI white baguette base.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat (5.7 g/100g) with low saturated fat (1.4 g/100g), but high sodium load raises cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The fat profile of this sandwich is relatively favourable with low saturated fat at 1.4 g/100g; however, the high sodium content (~1,200 mg per full serving) is a significant cardiovascular concern and individuals with CVD should limit portion size, reduce high-sodium condiments, and ensure this food is consumed within a low-sodium dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Cake Sandwich (Bánh Mì Chả Cá) is a Vietnamese-style baguette sandwich sold in Singapore, featuring homemade fish cake (chả cá) as the primary protein filling. The baguette is baked in the French-Vietnamese tradition — crisp on the outside, soft inside — and is filled with pan-fried or steamed fish cake, exclusive fish sauce as a condiment, pickled carrots and daikon (đồ chua), fresh laksa leaves (Vietnamese mint / rau răm), cilantro, fresh vegetables, and sliced chilli. The dish reflects the Vietnamese bánh mì tradition, which fuses French colonial baguette culture with Southeast Asian flavours and ingredients. Per 100 g, the sandwich provides approximately 157 kcal, 7.1 g protein, 5.7 g fat (1.4 g saturated), and 18.6 g carbohydrate with only 1.1 g dietary fibre, reflecting the refined white flour baguette base. Sodium is notable at 342.9 mg/100g, rising to approximately 1,200 mg per full 350 g serving due to fish sauce and pickled vegetables. The glycemic index is estimated at 70 (high) based on the white baguette component. Clinically, this food is a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its high-GI refined carbohydrate load, and for those with hypertension due to its high sodium content. The fish cake contributes lean protein and the herbs provide polyphenols and vitamin C (4.3 mg/100g). This sandwich is a popular street-food-style lunch option in Vietnamese cuisine communities in Singapore.

How many calories in a fish cake banh mi?
Is banh mi suitable for diabetics?

What is chả cá in a sandwich?

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source (HPB Singapore, USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA) contains a direct entry for Vietnamese fish cake sandwich (bánh mì chả cá). Nutrient values are derived from the input record (ai_generated, confidence 0.7) and cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central component entries for baguette and fish cake products. The GI is an analogous estimate based on white French baguette GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence reflects the composite estimation approach and the absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this Vietnamese fish cake sandwich composite is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not available for this composite dish in any approved primary source consulted (USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore, FAO/INFOODS SEA) as of 2024. 3. The input data source (GrabFood menu listing) is a forbidden primary source under Project Veritas v2 source policy; it has been replaced with USDA FoodData Central as the approved reference anchor. 4. Nutrient values from the input record have been preserved as the best available estimate and cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central component entries. 5. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on white French baguette GI per University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. (2008). 6. Halal status assessed as likely halal given fish-based filling and no pork/alcohol ingredients declared; this should be confirmed with the specific vendor. 7. trans_fat_g of 0.03 g/100g is retained from the input record as a trace value consistent with USDA data for baked goods. 8. This dish is sold in Singapore and is Vietnamese in cuisine; multilingual names have been provided for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to support Singapore multilingual retrieval, in addition to the mandatory Vietnamese native-script name. Chinese names are translational renderings (鱼饼三明治 / 魚餅三明治); Malay name is Sandwic Kek Ikan; Tamil name is மீன் கேக் சாண்ட்விச்; Hindi name is मछली केक सैंडविच.
